title: A Shipment's Risk Journey
decision: understand where intervention most reduces shipment risk
prompt:
Create a seven-slide scrollytelling-style deck for supply-chain leaders following one shipment from booking through delivery. Each slide should advance the same journey, reveal one new risk signal, and preserve a coherent static PowerPoint fallback. Use the supplied event sequence exactly. Include an editable progression timeline, cumulative risk chart, handoff diagram, exception evidence, and final intervention decision. Do not fabricate a live interaction or animation in the PPTX.

Design direction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

designDirection.label: Evidence editorial
designDirection.instruction: Use an evidence-led editorial direction: restrained surface treatment, decisive hierarchy, visible provenance, and an inspectable visual argument on every proof slide. Avoid card grids and decorative dashboards.

trace.summary: The user consented to send the full brief and 1 uploaded data source to OpenRouter. The named Claude Sonnet 5 model supplied the narrative plan through pi-ai; NodeSlide normalized, persisted, and validated the deck deterministically. Self-critique: 1 pass, clean.
trace.metrics: RUN TIME 1s TOKENS 2 → 4,739 COST $0.0528 VALIDATION Passed
